organizations.notes: FEC records compiled by City-Data list 'Mr. Robert B. Barnes', employer International Bank of Comme[r]ce, occupation President & CEO, Lakeway TX zip 78734 — an exact zip match to this donor — giving $500 to Texans for Senator John Cornyn in 2014, consistent with the donor's 58 prior strongly Republican-leaning federal contributions. IBC's own leadership page carries his full bio: 50+ years in Texas banking, Chairman & CEO of IBC Bank-Austin, U.S. Army Vietnam veteran, Randolph-Macon BA and University of Arkansas MBA. Now reports as RETIRED.

Federal contribution records repeatedly show Stuart Shaw at Austin TX 78735 — an exact zip match to the D1 filing — with employer 'Bonner Carrington' and occupation CEO/President, and a 2020 record listing occupation 'Retired' at the same 78735 address, matching the filing's 'RETIRED'. Bonner Carrington's own leadership page identifies Stuart Shaw as Founder & CEO with 40 years in commercial real estate, original developer of Austin's Warehouse District and Second Street Retail District, and builder of 4,000+ apartment homes across twenty Texas communities. The unusually high federal donation count (2,941) is consistent with his FEC record: 36 transactions in the 2016 cycle and 221 in the 2020 cycle alone, dominated by small recurring NRSC/WinRed contributions.

Ellen Wood founded vcfo in Austin in 1996 and is its CEO; a UT graduate and registered Texas CPA who has spent her whole career in Austin. Filing occupations 'CEO/CFO' and employer 'vcfo/VCFO' match her firm bio exactly. Her full bio documents an extensive Austin business-civic record (Austin Chamber past chair, Opportunity Austin board, Texas Business Leadership Council). No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found and no PAC/ideological affiliations on any tracked spectrum (Israel/Palestine, guns, oil & gas, defense) surfaced in public records; her documented affiliations are business/economic-development civic bodies.

organizations.org: Data Foundry / Golden Frog / Giganews / Texas.Net
organizations.notes: 'C M Yokubaitis' at zip 78746 is Carolyn M. Yokubaitis of Austin, who with her husband Ron Yokubaitis co-founded Texas.Net (one of the first 50 U.S. ISPs, 1994), Giganews, Golden Frog (VyprVPN) and the Austin data-center company Data Foundry, where she served as co-CEO. Federal contribution records list the identical contributor string 'C M YOKUBAITIS' at zip 78746 with employer/occupation 'DATA FOUNDRY/CEO', matching this donor's name and zip exactly; 'RETIRED' on the current filing is consistent with Data Foundry's 2021 sale to Switch. The second Yokubaitis donor at the same zip in this batch is the household match (Ron Yokubaitis, same 78746 address).

Michael Searle is a documented Austin political consultant and former City Hall staffer: aide/chief of staff to District 8 Council Member Ellen Troxclair, then head of the pro-Prop K PAC Citizens for an Accountable Austin and of the nonprofit Austin Civic Fund that funded it, and a City of Austin Tourism Commission appointee. The filing's 'CEO / Aro Group, LLC' is consistent with a political-consulting LLC ('The Aro Group Limited Liability Company' is registered in Austin, TX), but the direct link between the filing's employer string and this individual is not independently corroborated, and the 78745 zip was not confirmed against a public address — hence medium rather than high. FEC individual-contribution search returned no ideological-PAC giving (pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, gun, oil & gas, or defense); no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found under Searle, Aro Group, or Searle Strategies.

organizations.org: American Medical Association / Texas Medical Association / Travis County Medical Society / Surgical Society of Austin / Texas Transplant Society
organizations.role: Member (professional society memberships; no board or leadership role found)
organizations.notes: Dr. Stephen M. Settle, MD, FACS, vascular and endovascular surgeon at Cardiothoracic and Vascular Surgeons (CTVS Texas), 1010 W 40th St, Austin. Employer string 'CTVS' and occupation 'Physician' match exactly. Originally from Wellington, TX; MD from UT Southwestern Medical School (1986); general surgery internship/residency at St. Paul Medical Center, Dallas; vascular surgery residency at Baylor College of Medicine, Houston; in Austin practice since 1992 and at CTVS since 1999. Austin residential zip 78746 (Westlake) is consistent with an established Austin surgeon.

organizations.org: Open-internet / ISP-encryption policy advocacy (public press conference with Sen. Ted Cruz)
organizations.role: Speaker, as Golden Frog co-CEO, at Sen. Ted Cruz's open-internet press conference
organizations.notes: Ronald B. Yokubaitis, Austin TX 78746 — FEC records list him at Austin, TX 78746 with employer 'Data Foundry', occupation 'Founder', matching the donor zip exactly; State Bar of Texas lists Ronald B. Yokubaitis at a Bee Caves Rd address in 78746. He and wife Carolyn co-founded Texas.net (1994, one of the first 50 US ISPs), Giganews, Data Foundry and Golden Frog (VyprVPN). Now 82 and listed as RETIRED on the D1 filing, consistent with the post-sale exit from Data Foundry.

organizations.org: Save Austin Now / Save Austin Now PAC
organizations.notes: Co-founder (with Republican consultant Matt Mackowiak) of Save Austin Now and Save Austin Now PAC, the group behind 2021's Prop B homeless-camping-ban campaign and subsequent pro-police-funding advocacy; appointed by CLEAT (Combined Law Enforcement Associations of Texas) Executive Director Charley Wilkison to the Texas Commission on Law Enforcement (TCOLE) Advisory Committee in Nov. 2022. Self-describes as a lifelong Democrat and former probation officer/victim advocate; has written on Austin public-safety/homelessness policy for National Review, Austin American-Statesman, and The National Pulse. Zip 78735 Austin matches.

organizations.role: Travis County Commissioner, Precinct 3, 2002-08 & 2013-2020; described in press coverage as 'Travis County's last Republican' commissioner
organizations.notes: Zip 78735 matches the Austin home address (1403 Club Ridge Cove) on Gerald T. Daugherty's Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration, and occupation 'RETIRED' matches his December 2020 retirement from the Travis County Commissioners Court after 14 years as the county's sole Republican commissioner. He later became a restaurateur (Jack Allen's Kitchen) after decades as owner of Pleasant Valley Sportsplex. FEC/PAC-side searches (Club for Growth, NRA, Republican Party of Texas PAC, etc.) turned up general Republican-donor-space results but no specific PAC-board or PAC-leadership record tied to him personally beyond the existing 250-donation federal giving history already on file.

Company website, LinkedIn, and ZoomInfo confirm Chris Covo as President/Founder of Partners Commercial Cleaning, matching the donor's employer ('Partners Commercial Cleaning, LLC') and occupation ('PRESIDENT'). Prior to founding the company he was student body president at Texas State University (2009-10, per multiple secondary bios) and, per a 2010 Texas Senate committee witness-biography document filed under his name, served as Director of Young Americans for Prosperity in Texas. No affiliation found for spouse Amanda Covo specifically.

organizations.org: Citizens for an Accountable Austin PAC / Austin Civic Fund
organizations.role: PAC head / Executive Director (chair of the funding nonprofit)
organizations.notes: Filing lists occupation/employer as "SELF"/"CEO" (fields appear swapped, i.e. self-employed CEO) — a distinctive match to Michael Searle, an Austin political consultant who founded and runs Searle Strategies, a public-affairs firm, after serving as an aide to conservative Council Member Ellen Troxclair. He headed "Citizens for an Accountable Austin," the pro-Prop K PAC behind the 2018 city-efficiency-audit ballot measure, funded via roughly $137,000 from the dark-money nonprofit Austin Civic Fund, which he also led as Executive Director/chair; he declined to disclose the fund's donors, prompting a city Ethics Commission review of a possible dark-money-ordinance violation. Austin Chronicle describes him as a leader of ALEC's (American Legislative Exchange Council) local-government affiliate, which seeks partners to privatize municipal services. He also serves on the boards of the Austin Independent Business Alliance and Just Liberty (bipartisan criminal-justice reform) and on the City of Austin Tourism Commission. Identity confidence is medium: the CEO/self-employed occupation pairing is a strong, distinctive match to this public figure, but the Dripping Springs (78620) address was not independently corroborated in the sources found.

organizations.role: County Commissioner, 1995–2026 (retired); previously Travis County Constable from 1980
organizations.notes: Identity confirmed by donation-history continuity at a single address in City of Austin campaign finance data: 'Gomez, Margaret', Austin TX 78704 / 78704-5950, listed as occupation 'County Commissioner', employer 'Travis County' on contributions in 2024 (to Gary Bledsoe) and 2025-12-03 (to Marc Duchen), then as 'Retired' on the 2026-04-07 $450 contribution to Steven A. Brown — matching her 2026 retirement. This is Margaret Gómez, the longest-serving commissioner in Travis County history, first elected commissioner in 1995 and the first Mexican-American woman in the role; she stepped down in June 2026 after nearly 53 years as a county employee. Zip 78704 is consistent with Precinct 4, which covers nearly all of South Austin east of South Lamar. Not listed in Texas Ethics Commission registered-lobbyist lists for 2021–2026. The project's FEC match shows lean 1.0 (Democratic) across 928 federal donations, consistent with a longtime Democratic officeholder's recurring small-dollar giving; specific committees could not be enumerated this session because the OpenFEC API returned HTTP 429 on every attempt, and no PAC ties in any tracked category surfaced via web search.

Best match is Gary Allan Cobb, longtime Travis County assistant district attorney (24 years, director of the Grand Jury/Intake Division), UT Law 1986, who ran in the 2008 and 2016 Democratic primaries for Travis County DA and said after losing to Margaret Moore in 2016 that he would not stay at the office. Zip 78749 is corroborated by contemporaneous reporting describing him as vice president of the Southwest Austin Democrats (78749 is southwest Austin) and by a City of Austin filing listing a 512-899 (southwest Austin) home number. 'Retired' occupation and a heavily Democratic 92-donation federal giving history (FEC lean 0.90) are consistent with his documented Democratic-primary political activity. Confidence held at medium because no public record directly ties the 78749 address to this specific Gary Cobb, and there are other Austin-area Gary Cobbs (including one whose obituary appears in 2019). Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile and Martindale/State Bar listings found no lobbying, energy, firearms, defense, or Israel/Palestine-related roles on any side; no TEC lobbyist registration found.

Ora Houston is the first person to hold the Austin City Council District 1 seat (2015-2019), a lifelong resident of the Blackland neighborhood in East Austin (zip 78722). Occupation 'Retired' and the 78722 zip match her documented residence; her $400 gift to Steven Brown is in the District 1 seat she formerly held. No FEC ideological-PAC contributions and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration surfaced.

Confirmed as former Austin City Council Member Laura Morrison: FEC contribution records list Laura Morrison at Austin ZIP 78756 with employer 'Texas Electric Transportation Resource' and occupation 'Director of Community Initiatives,' and TxETRA's own staff page identifies that Laura Morrison as the former Lockheed engineer who 'served two terms on the Austin City Council.' Her name no longer appears on the successor org's current team page, consistent with 'RETIRED' on the 2026 filing.

Reported occupation 'Director' and employer 'PODER' match exactly a well-documented East Austin public-record figure: Susana Almanza co-founded PODER in 1991 and serves as its director. She is a longtime environmental-justice organizer, a two-time Austin City Council District 3 candidate (2014, 2018), and a 2021 appointee to the National Environmental Justice Advisory Council. Her documented advocacy is environmental justice, anti-gentrification and land use in East Austin; no federal PAC, lobbyist-registry, or Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas, or defense-industry affiliations were found on either side of those spectrums.

organizations.org: Carmen Llanes Pulido for Mayor of Austin (2024)
organizations.notes: Carmen Llanes Pulido, native East Austinite (donor zip 78722 is East Austin), has been Executive Director of Go Austin/Vamos Austin (GAVA) since March 2017 and ran for Mayor of Austin in 2024, finishing second with about 20% of the vote. Filing employer/occupation ('Go Austin Vamos Austin (GAVA)', 'Executive Director / Nonprofit Executive Director') matches exactly. She previously organized with PODER (People Organized in Defense of Earth and her Resources) in East Austin and ran a fitness/nutrition program at Marathon Kids. FEC search returned no federal PAC contributions under her name; her documented political finance activity is her local Austin mayoral committee (~$71,000 raised through July 2024), which is city-level, not federal. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration under 'Llanes' was found. Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile, InfluenceWatch and UT School of Architecture event bio found extensive local civic and land-use advocacy but no Israel/Palestine, gun, oil-and-gas or defense affiliations on either side of those spectrums.

Brown. The employer+occupation match Dr. Robert E. Askew Jr., MD, a general/robotic/laparoscopic surgeon who joined his father's Austin practice in 1991 and practices at Austin Surgeons (3901 Medical Pkwy). Note: the project's FEC match records a lean of 0.0 (Republican-leaning) across 13 federal donations for this household; the specific committees could not be re-verified this session because the FEC OpenFEC API returned HTTP 429 (rate limit) on every attempt, and no PAC ties in any tracked category surfaced via web search. Erin Askew could not be independently identified — several unrelated Erin Askews exist in Austin — so no separate occupation is asserted for her.

organizations.org: Bob Bullock Texas State History Museum Foundation
organizations.notes: FEC records compiled by City-Data list 'Mr. Robert B. Barnes', employer International Bank of Comme[r]ce, occupation President & CEO, Lakeway TX zip 78734 — an exact zip match to this donor — giving $500 to Texans for Senator John Cornyn in 2014, consistent with the donor's 58 prior strongly Republican-leaning federal contributions. IBC's own leadership page carries his full bio: 50+ years in Texas banking, Chairman & CEO of IBC Bank-Austin, U.S. Army Vietnam veteran, Randolph-Macon BA and University of Arkansas MBA. Now reports as RETIRED.

Best match is Gary Allan Cobb, longtime Travis County assistant district attorney (24 years, director of the Grand Jury/Intake Division), UT Law 1986, who ran in the 2008 and 2016 Democratic primaries for Travis County DA and said after losing to Margaret Moore in 2016 that he would not stay at the office. Zip 78749 is corroborated by contemporaneous reporting describing him as vice president of the Southwest Austin Democrats (78749 is southwest Austin) and by a City of Austin filing listing a 512-899 (southwest Austin) home number. 'Retired' occupation and a heavily Democratic 92-donation federal giving history (FEC lean 0.90) are consistent with his documented Democratic-primary political activity. Confidence held at medium because no public record directly ties the 78749 address to this specific Gary Cobb, and there are other Austin-area Gary Cobbs (including one whose obituary appears in 2019). Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile and Martindale/State Bar listings found no lobbying, energy, firearms, defense, or Israel/Palestine-related roles on any side; no TEC lobbyist registration found.

Latreese Cooke is the founder and executive director of the MELJ Center, a Central Texas nonprofit providing reentry services for formerly incarcerated people; she also hosts 'Iron Sharpens Iron' on Austin's KAZI 88.7 FM, serves as Vice Chair of CAPCOG's Criminal Justice Advisory Committee, and is president of the Central Texas chapter of the National Action Network. Employer 'MELJ' and 'EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR' on the filing match exactly; Martindale (78655) is in the Austin/Central Texas area.

organizations.notes: Gina Houston of Austin is Chair of the City of Austin Arts Commission and a member of the Downtown Commission; she is publicly associated with the Blackland neighborhood in East Austin, consistent with the donor's 78722 ZIP and with giving to two District 1 candidates. organizations.notes: Ora Houston is the first person to hold the Austin City Council District 1 seat (2015-2019), a lifelong resident of the Blackland neighborhood in East Austin (zip 78722). Occupation 'Retired' and the 78722 zip match her documented residence; her $400 gift to Steven Brown is in the District 1 seat she formerly held. No FEC ideological-PAC contributions and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration surfaced.

Carmen Llanes Pulido, native East Austinite (donor zip 78722 is East Austin), has been Executive Director of Go Austin/Vamos Austin (GAVA) since March 2017 and ran for Mayor of Austin in 2024, finishing second with about 20% of the vote. Filing employer/occupation ('Go Austin Vamos Austin (GAVA)', 'Executive Director / Nonprofit Executive Director') matches exactly. She previously organized with PODER (People Organized in Defense of Earth and her Resources) in East Austin and ran a fitness/nutrition program at Marathon Kids. FEC search returned no federal PAC contributions under her name; her documented political finance activity is her local Austin mayoral committee (~$71,000 raised through July 2024), which is city-level, not federal. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration under 'Llanes' was found. Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile, InfluenceWatch and UT School of Architecture event bio found extensive local civic and land-use advocacy but no Israel/Palestine, gun, oil-and-gas or defense affiliations on either side of those spectrums.

Confirmed as former Austin City Council Member Laura Morrison: FEC contribution records list Laura Morrison at Austin ZIP 78756 with employer 'Texas Electric Transportation Resource' and occupation 'Director of Community Initiatives,' and TxETRA's own staff page identifies that Laura Morrison as the former Lockheed engineer who 'served two terms on the Austin City Council.' Her name no longer appears on the successor org's current team page, consistent with 'RETIRED' on the 2026 filing.

Art (Arthur G.) Olbert is a senior partner at FlexSkill Consulting LLC in Round Rock; public-records listings place Arthur G Olbert at a Round Rock 78681 address, matching the donor zip, and his consulting title matches the filed occupation 'Technology development and alliance management consultant' with employer 'FlexSkill'. He served as interim CTO of Intershop Communications AG through FlexSkill; the later 'RETIRED' occupation is consistent with his reported 1946 birth year. No FEC PAC contributions and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration surfaced; no affiliations found on any tracked policy spectrum.

organizations.org: Combined Law Enforcement Associations of Texas (CLEAT) / TCOLE Advisory Committee
organizations.notes: Co-founder (with Republican consultant Matt Mackowiak) of Save Austin Now and Save Austin Now PAC, the group behind 2021's Prop B homeless-camping-ban campaign and subsequent pro-police-funding advocacy; appointed by CLEAT (Combined Law Enforcement Associations of Texas) Executive Director Charley Wilkison to the Texas Commission on Law Enforcement (TCOLE) Advisory Committee in Nov. 2022. Self-describes as a lifelong Democrat and former probation officer/victim advocate; has written on Austin public-safety/homelessness policy for National Review, Austin American-Statesman, and The National Pulse. Zip 78735 Austin matches.

ATU Local 1091's own officer page lists Lawrence Prosser as Financial Secretary of the local, and GuideStar lists him as a principal officer of the Austin-based union (725 Airport Blvd, Austin TX). Exact match to the donor's stated occupation 'UNION OFFICER' and employer 'Amalgamated Transit Union Local 1091', Austin 78723. Local 1091 represents Capital Metro transit workers. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found; no ideological PAC ties surfaced on any side of the searched spectrums in FEC-record searches.

organizations.role: Former Chair of the Board of Directors
organizations.notes: Robin Rather, CEO of Austin-based sustainability consultancy Collective Strength, longtime Austin environmental activist and daughter of newsman Dan Rather. Occupation 'Consultant' / employer 'Self-employed' and zip 78704 match her published FEC contributor records (Robin Rather, CEO, Collective Strength, Austin 78704). Prior career as a tech-sector market research executive at IntelliQuest, CMP Media, and as CEO of Mindwave Research; ~25 years of market research and strategic planning work on 50+ climate, land conservation, water, transit, and regional planning projects. Her all-Democratic federal giving pattern (122 FEC records, lean 1.0) is consistent with her public profile.

organizations.role: Executive Director / head of the nonprofit
organizations.notes: Michael Searle is a documented Austin political consultant and former City Hall staffer: aide/chief of staff to District 8 Council Member Ellen Troxclair, then head of the pro-Prop K PAC Citizens for an Accountable Austin and of the nonprofit Austin Civic Fund that funded it, and a City of Austin Tourism Commission appointee. The filing's 'CEO / Aro Group, LLC' is consistent with a political-consulting LLC ('The Aro Group Limited Liability Company' is registered in Austin, TX), but the direct link between the filing's employer string and this individual is not independently corroborated, and the 78745 zip was not confirmed against a public address — hence medium rather than high. FEC individual-contribution search returned no ideological-PAC giving (pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, gun, oil & gas, or defense); no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found under Searle, Aro Group, or Searle Strategies.

organizations.org: Central Health performance-audit advocacy (Travis County Commissioners Court)
organizations.role: Public advocate / speaker before Commissioners Court
organizations.notes: Almost certainly Susan A. Spataro, CPA, who served ~24 years as Travis County Auditor until Travis County district judges removed her in August 2012; she is publicly described in Austin news coverage as 'retired Travis County Auditor' and remains an active Austin civic voice (Central Health audit advocacy). Corroborated by name + Austin location + 'RETIRED' occupation on the filing; the news sources do not state her zip, so identity is strong but not zip-confirmed.

Ellen Wood founded vcfo in Austin in 1996 and is its CEO; a UT graduate and registered Texas CPA who has spent her whole career in Austin. Filing occupations 'CEO/CFO' and employer 'vcfo/VCFO' match her firm bio exactly. Her full bio documents an extensive Austin business-civic record (Austin Chamber past chair, Opportunity Austin board, Texas Business Leadership Council). No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found and no PAC/ideological affiliations on any tracked spectrum (Israel/Palestine, guns, oil & gas, defense) surfaced in public records; her documented affiliations are business/economic-development civic bodies.

organizations.role: Recipient of the first Internet Community Leadership Award, later named the 'Ron Yokubaitis Internet Community Leadership Award'; internet-privacy and open-internet advocate
organizations.notes: Ronald B. Yokubaitis, Austin TX 78746 — FEC records list him at Austin, TX 78746 with employer 'Data Foundry', occupation 'Founder', matching the donor zip exactly; State Bar of Texas lists Ronald B. Yokubaitis at a Bee Caves Rd address in 78746. He and wife Carolyn co-founded Texas.net (1994, one of the first 50 US ISPs), Giganews, Data Foundry and Golden Frog (VyprVPN). Now 82 and listed as RETIRED on the D1 filing, consistent with the post-sale exit from Data Foundry.
